mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in CPIAUCSL over 3 months signals increasing inflation expectations, which investors price into nominal Treasury yields, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward yield change is not positively correlated with CPIAUCSL 3-month change (Pearson r < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, it signals labor market slack that depresses wage growth and aggregate demand, leading the market to anticipate monetary easing and pushing 10-year yields lower over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In instances where UNRATE > 5.5%, 60-day forward yields do not decline more often than expected by chance (binomial test p > 0.10), or average yield change is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising PAYEMS (strong employment) and RSAFS (strong retail sales) indicate robust aggregate demand that lifts real GDP expectations, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 5 trading days as investors reprice growth risk.
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS change * RSAFS change) has no significant positive impact on 5-day forward yield changes in a linear model (t-stat < 1.96), or the combined signal generates Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising), further increases in FEDFUNDS compress the term premium as the Federal Reserve signals resolve to control inflation, leading to a fall in the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when FEDFUNDS has risen > 100bp over the prior 6 months, a further increase does not lead to negative 20-day yield changes more than 50% of the time (i.e., no signal).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDPC1 increases reflect expanding economic output that raises the natural rate of interest, pushing up long-term Treasury yields over the following quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: The correlation between GDPC1 quarterly change and subsequent 60-day yield change is not significantly positive (one-tailed test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in INDPRO greater than 1% signals a sharp acceleration in industrial production, which boosts commodity demand and inflation expectations, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Among months with INDPRO MoM > 1%, the subsequent 20-day yield change is not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.05), or the strategy produces a negative Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: When both UNRATE falls and FEDFUNDS rises simultaneously, it indicates the economy is overheating, prompting the market to anticipate aggressive tightening that ultimately slows growth and lowers yields over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines over 3 months while FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: The joint signal (UNRATE 3-month drop & FEDFUNDS 3-month rise) does not lead to negative average 20-day yield changes, or the hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing boom regime (HOUST above 1500), further increases in HOUST signal sustained demand that feeds into broader economic growth and raises the neutral rate, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when HOUST exceeds 1500, the correlation between HOUST changes and 5-day forward yield changes is not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or the signal generates negative returns in a simple long-short strategy.
